description:
You are a performance engineer who has just finished recording the frame-per-second (FPS) data of a graphics-heavy application.  
    1. A raw capture file already exists at **/home/user/perf/raw/fps_samples.log**.  
       • Every line of this file is a single positive integer (no leading or trailing spaces), representing the instantaneous FPS recorded at one sampling point.  
    2. Your goal is to produce a tiny, one-line summary report that states only the highest FPS value observed.  

    Required end state of the system
    --------------------------------
    • A directory **/home/user/perf/report** must exist (create it if necessary).  
    • Inside that directory, a new file **/home/user/perf/report/fps_summary.txt** must be created.  
    • The file must contain **exactly one line**, in the form  
      ```
      MAX_FPS=<number>
      ```  
      where <number> is the maximum integer found in /home/user/perf/raw/fps_samples.log.  
      ‑ There must be **no extra spaces** before or after the equals sign.  
      ‑ End the line with a single Unix newline (LF).  
    • Once the file is in place, display its contents to the terminal so that automated grading can capture the output.

    Constraints & tips
    ------------------
    • Achieve everything with a single compound or piped shell command if you like; no separate scripts are necessary.  
    • Do NOT modify the original fps_samples.log file.  
    • Only the MAX_FPS line should appear in fps_summary.txt; no header, comments, or blank lines.

    Verification details the grader will check
    ------------------------------------------
    1. The path /home/user/perf/report/fps_summary.txt exists.  
    2. The file’s sole, newline-terminated line matches the exact pattern `MAX_FPS=` followed by the correct integer.  
    3. The integer equals the maximum value present in /home/user/perf/raw/fps_samples.log.  
    4. The agent echoed that single line to standard output during execution.

    Hints (do not reveal to grader)
    -------------------------------
    • Commands like `sort`, `tail`, `awk`, or `printf` may be useful.  
    • Remember to create the report directory before writing the file if it does not exist.
truth:
<!-- Pre-populated raw data file -->
    <file path="/home/user/perf/raw/fps_samples.log"><![CDATA[
59
60
61
57
62
60
58
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected output file the agent must create -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/perf/report/fps_summary.txt"><![CDATA[
MAX_FPS=62
]]></expected_file>
